preamble: A common dialectal variation in Nahuatl involves the shift of the lateral affricate [tɬ] to the alveolar affricate [ts]. This is a defining characteristic of the 't' and 'ts' dialects of Nahuatl compared to the 'tl' dialects (like Classical Nahuatl).

examples.note: Shift found in many modern varieties where Classical 'tl' becomes 'ts'.

sources: Canger, U. (1980). Five Hundred Years of Nahuatl Dialectology.
Lastra de Suárez, Y. (1986). Las áreas dialectales del náhuatl moderno.
